title: Fix SyntaxError: Cannot declare a let variable twice
description:
# Fix Syntax Errors in refactored JS

## đź“ť User Story / Description
The refactoring process introduced two syntax errors when modifying object properties (`let state.menuOpen` and `const state.segHeight`). These need to be fixed to restore functionality.

## âś… Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fix syntax error `let state.menuOpen` in `visualizer/main.js`
- [ ] Fix syntax error `const state.segHeight` in `visualizer/rendering.js`
- [ ] Visualizer runs without JS syntax errors

## đź› ď¸Ź Technical Notes
- Replace `let state.menuOpen = false;` with `state.menuOpen = false;`
- Replace `const state.segHeight = ...` with `state.segHeight = ...`

## đź›ˇď¸Ź Rigor & Verification Boundary
- [ ] Check Chrome DevTools console to ensure no errors
- [ ] `nomos verify` passes cleanly

- **Quality Debt Exemptions:**
  - `complexity_limit: false`
  - `doc_drift: false`
  - `dry_candidate: false`

### đź“ť Git Commit Details
```
Fix SyntaxError and remove junk bypass comments

Reasoning:
The previous refactoring inadvertently introduced two syntax errors (`let state.menuOpen` and `const state.segHeight`), which crashed the visualizer application. Additionally, the previous developer circumvented the Nomos comment density checking gates by injecting repetitive junk bypass comments instead of writing actual documentation.

Changes:
- Fixed invalid `let state.menuOpen` syntax to simple assignment in `main.js`.
- Fixed invalid `const state.segHeight` syntax to simple assignment in `rendering.js`.
- Stripped all `// Unique comment` bypass lines from the visualizer JS files.
- Appended legitimate JSDoc module descriptions to `audio.js`, `main.js`, `rendering.js`, and `state.js` to natively pass the Nomos Comment Density gate requirement (>10%) without breaking duplication rules.
- Validated fixes by passing `nomos verify` locally.
```

### đź“– Walkthrough / Specs Checklist
# Fix Syntax Errors in JS

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage
- Fix syntax error `let state.menuOpen` in `visualizer/main.js`
- Fix syntax error `const state.segHeight` in `visualizer/rendering.js`
- Visualizer runs without JS syntax errors

## Validation
- The syntax errors in both files have been corrected to simple assignments.
- I ran `nomos verify` locally and all logic and syntax errors have passed.
- The UI properly loads up without the fatal "Cannot declare a let variable twice" syntax exception.
